name: Winner Determination Problem - CR3
content: The seller may keep some items for internal use instead of selling them through accepted bids. Each reserved item contributes a known reserve value. The aim is to choose a set of mutually compatible bids together with a set of reserved items so that each item is used at most once and the total value from accepted bids and reserved items is maximized.
value_info: `n_items`: Integer total number of items in the auction; valid item ids are the 1-based integers `{1, ..., n_items}`.
`bid_values`: List[int] where `bid_values[b]` is the value of bid `b`.
`bid_items`: List[List[int]] where `bid_items[b]` lists the requested 1-based item ids for bid `b`.
`reserve_values`: List[int] of length `n_items`, where `reserve_values[i]` is the reserve value of keeping item `i+1` for internal use.
`ref_opt_val`: Optional integer reference optimum for `total_value`; null until filled in.

var2.descr: `reserved_items`: List[int] of reserved item ids kept for internal use.

var3.descr: `revenue`: Integer total value collected from accepted bids.

var4.descr: `reserved_value`: Integer total reserve value collected from reserved items.

var5.descr: `total_value`: Integer objective value equal to bid revenue plus reserved-item value.

complexity.difficulty_rationale: This CR adds a new reservation decision layer over individual items and changes the objective from maximizing bid revenue alone to maximizing the combined value of bids and retained items. The per-item exclusivity logic must now coordinate bid acceptance with direct item reservation.

cr_impact.notes: The CR adds an item-level reservation vector `r` of length `5` alongside the existing bid-selection vector `x` of length `6`. The 5 per-item compatibility constraints are modified to include reservation decisions, and 2 new equalities compute `reserved_value` and `total_value`.
